Although the offer receives a lot of traffic and receives names for very cheap, they wanted to test on the instant donation page to see if we can decrease our cost per donor and increase our conversion rate.
They believe that the combination of emotional appeal, direct questions, and emphasis on the threat to democracy, along with positioning Heritage as the solution, will make the treatment more compelling and result in a higher donor conversion rate compared to the control page.
experiments.keyLearnings:
The key learning from this experiment is that the treatment, which aimed to increase the donor conversion rate by using emotional appeal, direct questions, and emphasizing the threat to democracy, did not lead to a higher conversion rate compared to the control page.

The treatment saw a decrease of 22.1% in transactions for all traffic. However, the statistical level of confidence was only 75.0%, which means the results are not valid and should not be used for future decision making.

Based on this, it is clear that the treatment did not have the intended impact and may not resonate with the target audience. In future experimentation, it would be important to explore other strategies or messaging that can effectively increase the conversion rate.

It is also important to note that the experiment ran for 26 days, which is a significant duration. This indicates that the results are reliable in terms of the data collected. However, as the statistical level of confidence is below 85%, it is necessary to conduct further experiments to validate the findings and make informed decisions.

experiments.title: How using different images for a content offer acquisition funnel affects email conversion rate
experiments.researchQuestion: We believe that using an image of the ebook on the landing page for potential downloaders will achieve a higher email acquisition rate because users will perceive the ebook image as higher value.
experiments.summary:
Timeframe: 12/14/2023 - 01/16/2024
The Heritage Foundation is offering the “Guide to the Constitution” digital version as a free gift through paid Facebook ads, email prospecting and website traffic.
Heritage originally tested the concept of the laptop image vs the ebook image in a Dear Reader on daily signal. The results showed that click-through rate on the dear reader was 68.6% higher when using the ebook image vs no image at all. The laptop image produced a 30.1% decrease in clickthrough rate compared to the control.
The offer has always had the laptop image on the landing page, so they decided to test changing the image to the ebook to see if it would increase email acquisition rate on the landing page.
They believed it would because the ebook looks to have more value than just a picture of the offer through a laptop or phone screen.
experiments.keyLearnings:
The key learning from this experiment is that changing the image on the landing page from a laptop to an ebook did not result in a higher email acquisition rate. The treatment version actually saw a decrease in Transactions for All Traffic.

Although the results showed a decrease in Transactions, the statistical level of confidence was below 85%, which means that the results cannot be considered valid and should not be used for future decision making.

In future experimentation, it would be important to gather more data and increase the sample size in order to achieve a higher level of statistical confidence. It may also be worth considering other elements on the landing page, such as the layout, color scheme, or messaging, to determine if any of those factors have an impact on the email acquisition rate.

experiments.title: How a sticky bar on acquisition donation pages impacted conversions
experiments.researchQuestion: We believe that by adding a sticky bar for newly acquired names will achieve a higher conversion rate because the donation ask is enforced with year end motivations.
experiments.summary:
Timeframe: 11/30/2023 - 12/28/2023
Belmont Abbey College is a Catholic university located in North Carolina.
During calendar year-end, we decided to test the presence of a sticky bar on acquisition offer donation pages encouraging potential donors to make a year-end gift.
experiments.keyLearnings:
The key learning from this experiment is that adding a sticky bar with a persuasive message on acquisition offer donation pages can potentially increase the conversion rate. The treatment version, which included the sticky bar with a specific call-to-action and a motivational message, resulted in a 31.5% increase in transactions for all traffic.
However, it is important to note that the statistical level of confidence is below the desired threshold of 85%. This means that the results cannot be considered valid and should not be used for future decision making.
In future experimentation, it would be important to gather a larger sample size or run the experiment for a longer duration to increase the statistical power and achieve a higher level of confidence in the results. A higher level of confidence is essential to ensure that any changes made based on the experiment results are valid and reliable.
Additionally, it would be worth considering testing variations of the sticky bar message, design, or placement to find the most effective combination. Exploring different strategies and optimizing the implementation of sticky bars can potentially lead to improved conversion rates and better engagement with potential donors.
Overall, this experiment provides a valuable insight into the potential impact of adding a sticky bar on acquisition offer donation pages. However, further experimentation is needed to validate and refine the results before implementing any changes on a larger scale.

experiments.title: How using a campaign subject’s story to increase relevancy will affect donor conversion rates
experiments.researchQuestion: We believe that telling the story of Lottie Moon for donation page visitors will achieve an increase in conversion rate because it will give relevancy to the title of the campaign for people who might not be aware.
experiments.summary:
Timeframe: 12/18/2023 - 12/27/2023
The IMB was running their annual Lottie Moon Christmas Offering campaign. They knew that they had been growing their new donor file over the past few years, and were curious how they should position “Lottie Moon” (who was a former missionary).  On the campaign donation page, they decided to test their standard value proposition against one that told the story of Lottie Moon, in order to give more relevancy to the name of the campaign.
The control page identified the need, and the treatment page prefaced the need with the story of Lottie Moon. They split the traffic to determine a winner.
experiments.keyLearnings:
The key learning from this experiment is that telling the story of Lottie Moon on the donation page resulted in an increase in conversions. The treatment group, which received the Lottie Moon story, had a 27.2% higher conversion rate compared to the control group.
However, it is important to note that the statistical level of confidence for this result was only 76.5%, which is below the recommended threshold of 85%. This means that the results cannot be considered valid and should not be used for future decision making.
In future experimentation, it would be beneficial to collect more data and run the experiment for a longer period of time to increase the statistical level of confidence. Additionally, it may be helpful to make adjustments to the experiment design or implementation to minimize any potential confounding factors or biases.
Overall, while the initial results are promising, it is necessary to conduct further testing to fully validate the impact of telling the story of Lottie Moon on the donation page.

experiments.title: How increasing the perceived security on a campaign donation page impacts donation conversions
experiments.researchQuestion: We believe that adding a statement of security for the year end campaign donation page will achieve an increase in donor conversion because it is providing clarity of what the donor’s information is being used for.
experiments.summary:
Timeframe: 12/19/2023 - 01/03/2024
The Ashbrook Center ran a fundraising campaign during Calendar Year End. For this campaign, we wanted to see how increasing the perceived security of the donation page through value proposition impacted overall donor conversions.
For this test, we added a line of copy near the “Donate” button on the form that said, “We take data and privacy seriously. Your payment information is secure and will be used only to process your gift.” We also made the background of this section gray and added a lock icon to drive the secure message home.
experiments.keyLearnings:
The key learning from this experiment is that adding a statement of security to the donation page had a positive impact on donor conversion rates. The treatment group, which saw the added line of copy, experienced an 11.7% increase in One-Time Donations compared to the control group. However, the statistical level of confidence remained below the accepted threshold of 85%, indicating that the results may not be valid and should not be used for future decision making.
In future experimentation, it is important to conduct longer tests or gather a larger sample size to increase the statistical power and achieve a higher level of confidence. This will ensure more reliable and valid results. Additionally, it would be helpful to consider testing different variations of the security statement or explore other aspects that could increase donor trust and conversion rates, such as emphasizing the organization's track record of data protection or providing testimonials from previous donors.

experiments.title: How a Humanized Sticky Bar Impacts Conversion Rate on the Homepage
experiments.researchQuestion: We believe that a humanized sticky bar for users on the homepage will achieve an increase in donor conversion rate because donors are more likely to give to a real person than a general organizational mission..
experiments.summary:
Ended On: 01/04/2024
For RTB’s CYE Sticky Bar, we hypothesized that a humanized version from RTB’s President Fazale Rana would increase donor conversion rate. We believe that having the campaign mission come from a real person will allow for a better connection with the donor and help reinforce that these are not the goals of just an “organization” but individuals seeking to make an impact as well.
experiments.keyLearnings:
The key learning from this experiment is that the humanized version of the sticky bar, featuring language from the organization's President, did not result in an increase in donor conversion rate. In fact, the treatment version actually saw a decrease in donations compared to the control version. However, these results did not validate. However, they do prompt closer consideration of the language.
Does the change of "we" to "I" have a potential impact on how the statement is read for the donor? The goal of incorporating an image of Fuz was to humanize the mission, but it's possible that the copy inadvertently made the mission Fuz focused rather than communicating the broader goal.
It is important to continue experimenting and testing different strategies to optimize donor conversion rate. Future experiments could explore different messaging techniques or other elements of the website to find more effective ways to engage donors and increase conversions.

experiments.title: How Urgency Language and a Countdown Impacts Conversion Rate on a CYE Pop Up
experiments.researchQuestion: We believe that using urgency language and countdown for users on the homepage will achieve an increase in conversion rate because it underscores the limited window of time available for the donor to make their gift..
experiments.summary:
Ended On: 01/04/2024
For RTB’s CYE Pop-Up, we hypothesized that an added line of copy for urgency and a countdown time would increase donor conversion rate. The treatment iteration added “We urgently need your help.” to the copy as well as the dynamic countdown. Aside from these changes, the body copy remained the same.
experiments.keyLearnings:
The key learning from this experiment is that adding urgency language and a countdown to the homepage did not increase donor conversion rate, however the level of confidence was far below the amount needed for validity.
It should be noted that originally this popup was supposed to a include an end-of-year deadline for a matched gift opportunity. This was met shortly before the popup launched and the line about the match had to be removed. With this tangible deadline missing, it does prompt the question "What is urgent?" Yes, the mission stated in the copy is undoubtedly important, but does having a countdown on that mission of just 7 days end up seeming overreactive to the user?
In future experiments, it would be beneficial to run a countdown for a longer period and in a context that still has a clear deadline for the user to correlate alongside the timer.

experiments.title: How Adding a Humanized Sticky Bar Affects Giving
experiments.researchQuestion: We believe that adding a humanized touch for the cye sticky bar will achieve a higher donor conversion rate because it can make the donation process feel more personalized, a donor could see somebody that they regularly communicate with via email on the web.
experiments.summary:
Ended On: 01/05/2024
For this experiment, we wanted to see if we could increase donor conversion on a sticky bar by adding a picture of a popular email sender.
experiments.keyLearnings: By adding a picture to the sticky bar we were able to create a 4.8% lift in conversion. However, it was only at a 14.2% LOC

experiments.title: How the placement of a call-to-action impacts donation conversions
experiments.researchQuestion: We believe that placing the CTA up higher for website visitors will achieve an increase in donor conversion because  the CTA is more easily visible.
experiments.summary:
Timeframe: 12/15/2023 - 12/31/2023
America Needs Fatima has previously tested a slide out vs. a dear reader to feature different content offers. For this test, we wanted to test the slide out method for a direct appeal ask vs. a traditional sticky bar to see if the appeal placement impacted overall donation conversions.
For this test, both web elements contained the same copy and button CTA.
experiments.keyLearnings:
The key learning from this experiment is that placing the CTA higher on the website, using a slide out method, resulted in a 10.8% increase in one-time donations compared to a traditional sticky bar. However, the statistical level of confidence for this result is only 64.5%, which is below the accepted threshold of 85%. Therefore, these results cannot be considered valid and should not be used for future decision making.
In future experimentation, it is important to ensure that the statistical level of confidence is above 85% to have valid and reliable results. Additionally, it may be worth exploring other elements or factors that could impact donor conversions, such as the design or color scheme of the CTA button, the messaging used in the appeal, or the timing of the slide out or sticky bar. By testing these different elements and analyzing the results with a higher level of statistical confidence, the organization can make more informed decisions on how to optimize the website for increased donations.

experiments.title: How adding a donate button to the header and navigation menu affects donor conversion rate

rows.name: Donate Button in Header and Navigation Menu

experiments.researchQuestion: We believe that adding a donate button to the header and navigation menu for home page visitors will achieve an increase in donor conversion rate because more people will click to view the donation page. because more people will click to view the donation page.
experiments.summary:
Timeframe: 11/15/2023 - 12/11/2023
The Daily Signal is The Heritage Foundation’s multimedia news platform. On their website, the only call-to-action to donate and support the Daily Signal’s work is located at the very bottom in the footer of each page and doesn’t stand out.
In an attempt to increase the amount of traffic to the donation page from the home page, we wanted to test if adding a more prominently displayed donate button to the top of the home page led to an increase in donor conversion rate.
The donate button appeared in both the home page’s header and dropdown navigation menu (in both the desktop and mobile experiences) and was blue in color in order to stand out from the surrounding buttons.
experiments.keyLearnings:
This experiment ran for 27 days, in which time the donate button variant saw a 42.8% increase in donations for all traffic compared to the control. However, it is important to note that the statistical level of confidence for this increase is 53.2%, meaning that the results cannot be considered valid or reliable for future decision making.

In terms of revenue, the treatment saw a decrease of 0.11% in average gift, but at a 6.52% level of confidence these results cannot be relied upon to predict a valid outcome.

Reasons for this may include the donate button appearing less hierarchically prominent than the email "Sign Up" button above it, or home page visitors not yet having experienced the value in the Daily Signal's articles before seeing a donate button.

Based on the results, it is clear that additional testing is needed to understand what motivates The Daily Signal visitors to donate to the organization. This could include adding value proposition copy next to the button, or testing different locations on the home page. It may also be worth testing an unobtrusive sticky donate button that follows the visitor through their journey sitewide.

experiments.title: How a tabbed donation form affects donor conversion rates on a primary donation page
experiments.researchQuestion: We believe that a tabbed donation form for main donation page visitors will achieve an increase in overall donor conversion rates.
experiments.summary:
Timeframe: 11/27/2023 - 12/12/2023
We previously tested how a tabbed donation form affected recurring donations on a public policy organization’s main donation page. In this experiment, the tabbed donation form produced an 11% decrease in recurring gifts (with a 25% level of confidence) and a 25.4% decrease in overall donations (94.4% level of confidence), which disproved our hypothesis that a tabbed donation form would achieve higher recurring donation rates than a monthly giving checkbox by making the monthly giving option and its impact more visible.
In an effort to learn whether a tabbed donation form on this organization’s main donation page would lead to an increase in donor conversion rate, we retested this idea. The control in this experiment presents a monthly giving option as a checkbox below the gift array, while the treatment has two tabs for either a Single Gift or Monthly Gift with a value proposition callout box above the monthly gift tab. 50% of traffic saw the control and 50% saw the treatment.
experiments.keyLearnings:
After running this experiment for 16 days, we observed that the tabbed donation form on the main donation page led to an insignificant 2.2% increase in overall donor conversion rates, but its 11.3% level of confidence is not high enough to consider these results valid. Therefore, we cannot confidently conclude that the tabbed donation form is more effective in increasing conversions, but we also cannot conclude that it is less effective.
Additionally, the treatment saw a 99.5% increase in recurring donations, but at an invalid 58.4% level of confidence.
These results, along with data of tabbed donation forms leading to an increase in donations in the organization's acquisition offers funnels, may indicate that people visiting their main donation page may have high donor motivation and be less influenced or even more distracted by the tabbed design of the form. The results also may have been impacted by an additional variable—the control not having the value proposition callout box found in the treatment's experience.

experiments.title: How adding clarity around the nature of an offer using an image impacted clicks
experiments.researchQuestion: We believe that adding clarity with a digital image for housefile email recipients will achieve an increase in clickthrough rate because there will be enhanced clarity around the nature of the offer they are signing up for.
experiments.summary:
Timeframe: 11/25/2023 - 12/09/2023
When sending a Christmas devotional offer, DTS wanted to add clarity to potential readers that this was a digital offer and not a physical book since some of their other premiums are a physical copy of a particular resource. We set up a test to determine if additional clarity in the email would impact click rates from the housefile.
experiments.keyLearnings:
What we saw after running the experiment was the treatment that added a digital image for housefile email recipients resulted in a measured increase of only 0.1%, and it only reached a 1.3% level of confidence so the experiment results are not valid and the results are flat.

This will have to be tested on future content offers to get a better understanding of how housefile email recipients are motivated.

experiments.title: Does additional value prop drive results for a transactional type email?
experiments.researchQuestion: We believe that adding more value prop and missional language for email audience will achieve a higher conversion rate because the reason for the purchase is more thoroughly fleshed out. .
experiments.summary:
Ended On: 12/11/2023
Pathway to Victory is a broadcast ministry of First Baptist Dallas.
In November and December PTV has seen great response to two premiums, the 2024 Pathway to Victory Daily Devotional and You Can Pray Big Things.
In an effort to drive a bit more response, we decided to send an email encouraging people to make their last minute Christmas purchases by December 7th so that they will arrive in time to homes.
For this email, we decided to test a very short copy, straight to the point versus a longer copy with more missional and value prop language.
experiments.keyLearnings:
The key learning from this experiment is that the shorter, straight-to-the-point copy performed better than the longer copy with more missional and value prop language. Although the results are not statistically significant, there is a trend indicating that the shorter copy may be more effective in driving donations.

In future experimentation, it would be beneficial to further test the effectiveness of shorter, concise messaging in driving conversions. This could involve testing different variations of the short copy, or comparing it against other types of messaging, such as emotional appeals or storytelling.

Additionally, it is important to ensure that the statistical level of confidence in the results is sufficient before drawing any conclusions. Conducting tests with a larger sample size or running the experiment for a longer duration may help achieve a higher level of confidence in the results.

Overall, the key takeaway is that concise messaging may have a positive impact on conversion rates, and this should be further explored in future experimentation.

experiments.title: How does increasing the relevancy of a subject line affect email opens
experiments.researchQuestion: We believe that increasing the relevancy of a subject line for cultivation email recipients will achieve an increase in email engagement because the timeliness of the message is increased.
experiments.summary:
Ended On: 12/12/2023
The Navigators sent a cultivation email as apart of their calendar year end campaign that offered a Christmas Reading Plan for free to their housefile. The original subject line included exclusivity for the user as it said “A Christmas Reading Plan Just for You!” While we’ve seen exclusivity in copy perform well, we wanted to test how changing the subject line to be more timely to the message would affect email open rate. The new subject line read ” A must-read before Christmas!”
experiments.keyLearnings:
The key learnings from this experiment are:

1. Changing the subject line to be more timely and relevant to the message can lead to an increase in email engagement.

2. But while the treatment showed a slight increase in opens, the statistical level of confidence was below the threshold of 85%, indicating that the results are not valid.

To improve future experimentation, testing different variations of subject lines and analyzing the impact on email engagement can provide further insights. It is also crucial to track other engagement metrics such as click-through rate and conversion rate to get a holistic view of the success of email campaigns.

experiments.title: How adding clarity around the impact of a donation affects donor conversion rate
experiments.researchQuestion: We believe that adding clarity about the impact of their gift and the matching gift opportunity for subscribers will achieve an increase in donor conversion because  it provides more clarity and transparency, addressing potential concerns and building trust.
experiments.summary:
Ended On: 12/14/2023
ACI is running their CYE campaign. We wanted to test in the pro-life-focused email how adding clarity about the impact of their gift and the impact of the matching gift opportunity would affect the conversion rate. We did this by adding specific bullets about what their gift will do to help move ACI’s mission forward.
experiments.keyLearnings:
The key learning from this experiment is that adding clarity about the impact of their gift and the matching gift opportunity in the pro-life-focused email had a negative effect on the donor conversion rate. The treatment group, which received the email with the added clarity, saw a 21.2% decrease in donations compared to the control group.
However, it is important to note that the results of this experiment are not statistically significant, as the level of confidence is below the recommended threshold of 85%.
In future experimentation, it would be beneficial to conduct a more robust study with a larger sample size and a longer testing period to gather more reliable results. Additionally, it may be worth exploring different variations of the email content to see if there are other messaging strategies that can effectively communicate the impact of their gift and the matching gift opportunity without negatively affecting the conversion rate.

experiments.title: How using language around the impact of the matching gift affects donor conversion rate
experiments.researchQuestion: We believe that using language around the impact of the matching gift rather than simply stating it will be doubled  for ACI’s housefile will achieve an increase in conversion rate  because it provides more clarity and transparency, addressing potential concerns and building trust. because  it provides more clarity and transparency, addressing potential concerns and building trust.
experiments.summary:
Ended On: 12/14/2023
ACI is running their CYE campaign. They have a $100,000 matching grant for this campaign, so we wondered how using language around the impact of the matching gift rather than just saying their gift will have double the impact would affect donor conversion rate. We ran this test in an email to their full housefile.
experiments.keyLearnings:
The key learning from this experiment is that using language around the impact of a matching gift instead of simply stating that the gift will be doubled did not have an effect on donor conversion rate.
Because CYE is already a motivating time to give, we believe that the added context and clarity did not increase motivation to give because it was already so high.
In future experimentation, it would be wise to consider alternative approaches to communicate the impact of a matching gift. It may be beneficial to test different messaging strategies, such as emphasizing the urgency or importance of the campaign or highlighting specific examples of how the matching gift will be used to make a difference.

experiments.title: How removing a premium and specific ask amount from a donation page impacted donor conversion
experiments.researchQuestion: We believe that removing the premium of a pledge coin  for visitors will achieve an increase in donor conversion because it will remove unnecessary friction from the donation process.
experiments.summary:
Timeframe: 11/28/2023 - 01/04/2024
In an effort to increase our instant donor conversion rate for ACI, we wanted to test removing the premium of their 2023 Pledge Coin which required a $100 donation. We felt that the high ask amount and the premium was causing unnecessary friction for the user, causing them to give lower amounts or not at all.
Therefore, our hypothesis was that be removing the premium all together, potential donors would have a clearer idea of what was being asked of them due to the clarity now being provided.
experiments.keyLearnings:
The key learning from this experiment is that removing the premium of the pledge coin for visitors resulted in a 11.9% increase in the number of transactions. However, due to the statistical level of confidence being below 95%, these results cannot be considered valid.
The treatment saw a negative impact in revenue at a 51.7% drop with a 16.4% level of confidence, also not considered valid.
In future experimentation, it is important to conduct experiments for a longer duration to collect more data and increase the statistical confidence level. While you should not conduct future decision making off of these results, we can observe how donors perceived this change. That is, more people were willing to give when there was no premium, but the average gift fell significantly. Therefore, it would be crucial that you consider these results from a strategic standpoint of focusing on donors or donations.
Overall, this experiment highlights the potential of removing unnecessary friction in the donation process to improve donor conversion. It is recommended to continue exploring different strategies to simplify the donation experience and increase revenue for the American Cornerstone Institute.

experiments.title: How removing a signer’s image in an email footer signature impacted clicks

rows.name: Control - With Image in Footer Signature
Treatment - No Image in Footer Signature

experiments.researchQuestion: We believe that removing a photo from the email signature for housefile recipients will achieve an increase in clickthrough rate because the email will appear more human and personalized.
experiments.summary:
Timeframe: 12/06/2023 - 12/14/2023
When sending a course registration email, we wondered if the signature image was causing the email template to appear more like marketing and less like a personalized email coming from a real human. We split the file 50/50 and tested each version to measure engagement.
experiments.keyLearnings:
The key learning from this experiment is that removing a photo from the email signature for housefile recipients resulted in a 7.5% increase in clickthrough rate. However, the statistical level of confidence was only 79.8%, which means the results cannot be considered valid.
We will want to re-test this concept in future experiments to fully measure the impact of removing the photo from the email signature.

experiments.title: How a sticky donate button affects donor conversion rates on main pages on a website
experiments.researchQuestion: We believe that adding an additional opportunity to give for site visitors will achieve an increase in donations because they are being prompted to give and reminded how The Navigators has helped them by using value proposition language. because they are being prompted to give and reminded how The Navigators has helped them by using value proposition language.
experiments.summary:
Ended On: 11/21/2023
The Navigators wanted to take advantage of the traffic generated through their resource and blog pages. As people are being equipped/informed, we wanted to run a test where people would see a sticky donate button on the side that would prompt people to give. The treatment would slide out when someone scrolled halfway through the page. If they click “yes” they would be taken to a donation page to make their gift.
experiments.keyLearnings:
The key learning from this experiment is that adding an additional opportunity for site visitors to donate can potentially lead to an increase in donations. The treatment version, which had a sticky donate button that prompted visitors to give and reminded them of how The Navigators help others, saw a 28.0% increase in donations compared to the control version.

However, it is important to note that the statistical level of confidence for this result is only 70.2%, which is below the recommended threshold of 85%.

It would be beneficial to test different versions of the treatment to further optimize the effectiveness of the sticky donate button. By testing different variations and monitoring the impact on donations, the organization can continue to refine and improve their approach to increasing donations.

Overall, the key learning is that adding an additional opportunity for site visitors to donate can potentially have a positive impact on donations. However, further testing and optimization are needed to determine the most effective approach.

experiments.title: How using an eBook cover image on a landing page impacted email acquisition
experiments.researchQuestion: We believe that adding an eBook cover image for a free content offer will achieve an increase in email acquisition because there is additional clarity enhancing the perceived value of the offer on the page.
experiments.summary:
Timeframe: 11/22/2023 - 12/21/2023
When sharing a digital magazine issue, we wondered if adding clarity to the landing page by showing a preview of the magazine cover would motivate more people to convert on the email acquisition page. We developed a treatment that included the eBook image alongside the copy and split traffic 50/50.
experiments.keyLearnings:
The key learnings from this experiment are that adding an eBook cover image to a landing page for a free content offer did not result in an increase in email acquisition. In fact, the treatment saw a decrease in email acquisition compared to the control group. However, since the statistical level of confidence is below 95%, the results of this experiment cannot be considered valid and should not be used for future decision making.
In future experimentation, it would be beneficial to conduct further tests with a larger sample size and longer duration to obtain more reliable results. Additionally, it may be worth exploring other factors that could potentially increase email acquisition, such as different call-to-action copy or alternative content offers. Continuous testing and experimentation will help to identify what strategies and elements are most effective in attracting and converting users.

experiments.title: How a recurring donation interrupter affects recurring donor conversion rate
experiments.researchQuestion: We believe that using a donation interrupter to ask users to convert their one-time gift to a monthly gift for potential donors will achieve a higher recurring donor conversion rate because they will be forced to think of the impact and what they receive in return to convert the one-time gift.
experiments.summary:
Timeframe: 12/07/2023 - 01/05/2024
MSLF is running a petition through paid Facebook advertising, and they wanted to test how a donation pop-up interrupter would perform to increase the amount of recurring donors they get through acquisition efforts.
Once users finish the petition, they proceed to an instant donation page where they’re asked to make a gift to support this case and the work MSLF is doing. There are options to make a one-time or monthly gift on the donation form, but if the user chose one-time, they would receive a pop-up after hitting “Donate” asking them to convert their gift to a $10 monthly donation.
The pop-up included value proposition as to what they will receive if they make a monthly donation and become a member to MSLF.
experiments.keyLearnings:
The key learning from this experiment is that using a donation interrupter to ask users to convert their one-time gift to a monthly gift can significantly increase the conversion rate for recurring donations.
The treatment group experienced a 154.5% increase in recurring donation conversion rate, indicating that the interrupter was effective in prompting users to consider the impact of their donation and what they would receive in return as a member of the organization. However, it is important to note that the statistical level of confidence was only 75.1%, which falls below the recommended confidence level of 85%.
In addition, the experiment showed that the one-time donation rate decreased by 326% with a 97% level of confidence. This means that the interrupter influenced users to opt out of giving all together instead of making a one-time gift or recurring gift.
Future experimentation can involve refining the design and messaging of the interrupter to maximize its impact. It is also important to consider the potential impact of the interrupter on user experience and whether it could potentially deter users from making any donation at all. A/B testing with different variations of the interrupter can help in identifying the most effective approach.
Overall, the key learnings from this experiment highlight the potential of using a donation interrupter to increase recurring donations. However, further experimentation and validation are needed to make informed decisions and implement effective strategies.

experiments.title: How a larger impact number affects donor conversion on the main donation page
experiments.researchQuestion: We believe that showing a larger impact number for main donation page visitors will achieve an increase in conversion rate because it will increase their perception of Heritage’s impact.
experiments.summary:
Timeframe: 10/18/2023 - 11/06/2023
The Heritage Foundation had long used their 500k member base as a leg of their value proposition to show their broad support. However, their marketing team’s outreach initiatives meant that they actually reached a lot more people each year than they had members.
Donors responded very favorably to this “larger” metric, but they wanted to see if that proved out in increased fundraising results.
experiments.keyLearnings:
The key learning from this experiment is that by showing a larger impact number for main donation page visitors, the conversion rate can be increased. This is supported by the fact that the treatment version, which presented the larger number of 10 million members, advocates, and concerned Americans reached per day, resulted in a 7.7% increase in transactions for all traffic.

However, it is important to note that the statistical level of confidence for this result is only 34.4%, which is below the recommended threshold of 85%. Therefore, the results cannot be considered valid and should not be used for future decision making.

Based on this, it is recommended to conduct further experiments to validate the findings and determine the true impact of showing a larger number on the conversion rate. The future experimentation should involve a larger sample size and longer duration to ensure more reliable results. Additionally, it may be beneficial to test variations in the language used to present the impact number to see if different phrasing or positioning can further improve the conversion rate.

experiments.title: How a large image on a landing page affects donor conversion
experiments.researchQuestion: We believe that removing the hero image for Holy Lands donation page visitors will achieve an increase in conversion rate because it will reduce visual friction on the page.
experiments.summary:
Ended On: 10/31/2023
The IMB was running a campaign to raise funds for mission work in the Holy Lands during the Israel-Hamas conflict in 2023. Motivation to give was high for the campaign, reflected in high conversion rates even from direct acquisition.
When looking for opportunities to optimize conversion, the team wondered if the image on the landing page — although striking — might actually be pushing the rest of the content too far down on the page. It was large on the desktop version, and pushed all the copy, value proposition, and donation form below the initial “fold”.
They quickly mocked up an experiment that removed the image and launched an A/B test to determine a winner.
experiments.keyLearnings:
The key learning from this experiment is that removing the hero image from the Holy Lands donation page resulted in a 9.5% increase in donations. However, the statistical level of confidence is below the recommended threshold of 85%, so these results cannot be considered valid and should not be used for future decision making.
Based on this, it is important to recognize that the experiment did show a potential impact on conversions by removing the hero image. This suggests that reducing visual friction on the page could be beneficial in improving conversion rates.
To further validate these findings and make informed decisions, future experimentation should be conducted with a larger sample size and higher statistical level of confidence. This will help ensure the accuracy and reliability of the results.
Additionally, it is recommended to explore other potential optimizations on the landing page to further improve conversion rates. This could involve testing different layout designs, call-to-action placements, or content positioning to find the most effective approach for encouraging donations.
Overall, the learnings from this experiment highlight the importance of conducting rigorous and statistically valid experiments to make informed decisions in future optimization efforts.

experiments.title: How adding a gift array in the body of an email appeal impacted clicks
experiments.researchQuestion: We believe that introducing a sample gift array for a direct email appeal will achieve an increase in engagement because there will be additional cognitive momentum to continue the process to the next step.
experiments.summary:
Timeframe: 11/27/2023 - 11/28/2023
In a high-urgency campaign, we typically keep the decisions isolated per each step. In the email step, we help the reader decide “Do I want to click?” and then at the donation page step we help the reader decide “How much do I want to give?” In one email, we wanted to test if adding gift array options at the email level would help more people begin thinking about both questions at once. The treatment version added a gift array of buttons, letting the reader know they could click one of the buttons to choose their donation amount and begin the process of making a monthly gift.
experiments.keyLearnings: After splitting the email between the control and the treatment, we observed a 1.2% lift in clicks at 9.4% level of confidence for the treatment version which is not statistically significant so the experiment results are not valid. The engagement remained flat, even when we added buttons with gift array amounts in the body of the email. We will want to look downstream and see if there is any significant difference in completed donations from the treatment group that would lead to more meaningful results to see if moving the gift array into the body copy helped produce more motivated visits from email traffic who had already decided what amount they would be interested in giving and continue the cognitive momentum in the process when they arrive on the donation page.

experiments.title: How increasing the perceived security on a campaign donation page impacts donation conversions
experiments.researchQuestion: We believe that adding a statement of security for the year end campaign donation page will achieve an increase in donor conversion because it is providing clarity of what the donor’s information is being used for.
experiments.summary:
Timeframe: 12/12/2023 - 01/04/2024
Luther Seminary ran a fundraising campaign during Calendar Year End. For this campaign, we wanted to see how increasing the perceived security of the donation page through value proposition impacted overall donor conversions.
For this test, we added a line of copy below the “Donate” button on the form that said, “We take data and privacy seriously. Your payment information is secure and will be used only to process your gift.” We also made the background of this section gray and added a lock icon to drive the secure message home.
experiments.keyLearnings:
The key learning from this experiment is that adding a statement of security to the donation page for the year-end campaign resulted in a 14.2% increase in transactions for all traffic. However, the statistical level of confidence was only 62.5%, which means that these results cannot be considered valid or reliable for future decision making.
In future experimentation, it is important to increase the sample size and run the experiment for a longer duration to obtain more reliable results. Additionally, it would be beneficial to test other variations of the value proposition statement to further improve donor conversion rates. This could involve testing different wording, design elements, or positioning of the security message. By continuing to experiment and refine the messaging and design of the donation page, it is possible to achieve a higher level of donor conversion and improve the overall success of fundraising campaigns.

experiments.title: How lowering a gift array on an instant donation page for an acquisition offer affects donor conversion rate
experiments.researchQuestion: We believe that lowering the gift array for note of encouragement signers will achieve a higher donor conversion rate because the lower gift array aligns better with the perceived value of the offer.
experiments.summary:
Ended On: 11/21/2023
IMB is running an acquisition offer through paid Facebook ads to sign a note of encouragement for missionaries who are traveling and out in the field. They wanted to increase the donor conversion rate on the instant donation page of the acquisition offer so we decided to lower the gift array because this is a low value offer and they aren’t receiving in return like an ebook funnel.
experiments.keyLearnings:
The key learning from this experiment is that lowering the gift array for note of encouragement signers did not achieve a higher donor conversion rate. In fact, the treatment version with the lower gift array saw a significant decrease in donations of 49.3% compared to the control version.
Based on these results, it is evident that the perceived value of the offer did not align with the lower gift array. This suggests that donors may be more willing to donate higher amounts when presented with a wider range of options. Or it suggests that users see this content offer as high value and makes them want to give more to support more missionaries.
In future experimentation, it is important to consider the perceived value of the offer and ensure that the gift array aligns with this value. It may be beneficial to test different gift array options to find the optimal balance between a wide range of options and the perceived value.

experiments.title: How using a more concise donation page in a high urgency campaign affects donor conversion
experiments.researchQuestion: We believe that adding clarity and being more concise for potential donors will achieve a higher donor conversion rate because repeated information on a donation page is added friction to the donation process which can deter people from giving.
experiments.summary:
Ended On: 11/21/2023
This ministry organization ran a high-urgency campaign to their full housefile with the goal of acquiring more recurring donors. The original donation page re-explained most points that the emails had already described, so we asked ourselves the questions:
Are subscribers still needing this much information by the time they get to the donation page? Or are they just ready to give, and we are getting in their way by proposing more hypothetical questions?
That’s why we proposed a second, short, to-the-point donation page that focuses on clarity and is more concise.
experiments.keyLearnings:
The key learning from this experiment is that a more concise and clear donation page may lead to a higher donor conversion rate. The treatment, which focused on eliminating repeated information and reducing friction in the donation process, saw a 0.9% increase in donations. However, the statistical level of confidence was only 4.8%, which is below the recommended 85%.
In future experimentation, it is important to continue exploring the impact of clarity and conciseness on donor conversion rates. The results of this experiment suggest that potential donors may prefer a streamlined and simplified donation process without excessive information repetition. Further testing with larger sample sizes and higher statistical power could help validate these findings and provide more reliable insights for decision making.
Additionally, it would also be valuable to explore other potential factors that could impact donor conversion rates, such as the design and layout of the donation page, messaging and storytelling techniques, and ease of use. By combining multiple variables and testing different combinations, a deeper understanding of the most effective strategies for acquiring recurring donors can be gained.

experiments.title: Does mentioned a previous text message impact conversions?
experiments.researchQuestion: Does removing the language mentioning a text message impact conversions?
experiments.summary:
Ended On: 11/27/2023
Pathway to Victory is a broadcast ministry of First Dallas.
A few weeks ago we tested how an email following a text message impacted over all conversions, https://app.winstonknows.com/experiment/6675.
As a follow up to this test we wanted to test the opening paragraph of the follow up email. Does the mention of the text message received impact conversions or is it just the presence of the follow up email with the same offer?
In the previous experiment above we did mention the text message therefore this test is removing the messaging to see if conversions are impacted.
experiments.keyLearnings:
The key learning from this experiment is that removing the language mentioning a text message did not have a significant impact on conversions. Although the treatment saw a 3.3% increase in donations for all traffic, the statistical level of confidence was only 10.8%, which is below the threshold of 85% for results to be considered valid.

Therefore, it is important not to draw any definitive conclusions from this experiment. In future experimentation, it would be beneficial to conduct further tests with larger sample sizes or different variations to validate the results and gain more reliable insights. Additionally, it would be useful to explore other factors that could potentially impact conversions, such as the content of the follow-up email or the timing of the communication.

experiments.title: How a headline on a donation page impacted conversions
experiments.researchQuestion: We believe that by adding a headline on the main donation form for all traffic will achieve a higher conversion rate because there is a clear call to action and reason for the donation.
experiments.summary:
Timeframe: 11/10/2023 - 11/17/2023
WUNC is North Carolina’s largest NPR station.
In an effort to convert donors, we decided to test adding a headline to WUNC’s main donation page during their Fall Drive. All traffic from email, advertising and web point to this page.
We did experience a delay in getting the experiment live due to technical issues, so we did miss the first 4 days of the drive which historically have the most traffic.
experiments.keyLearnings:
The key learning from this experiment is that adding a headline to the main donation form for all traffic did not achieve a higher conversion rate. In fact, the treatment group experienced a decrease in donations compared to the control group. However, due to technical issues and missing the first 4 days of the drive which historically have the most traffic, the results cannot be considered valid.
In future experimentation, it is important to ensure that technical issues are resolved promptly to avoid missing significant portions of the experiment. Additionally, it would be beneficial to conduct experiments for a longer duration to gather more accurate and comprehensive data.
Based on the inconclusive results of this experiment, it would be wise to test other variations and elements on the main donation page to identify factors that may lead to a higher conversion rate. It is essential to continue experimenting and iterating on the donation page to optimize its effectiveness in converting donors.

experiments.title: How adding a second link in an email impacted conversion for nondonors
experiments.researchQuestion: We believe that by adding a second link for email subscribers will achieve a higher conversion rate because there are more opportunities to click . because there are more opportunities to click .
experiments.summary:
Timeframe: 10/31/2023 - 11/03/2023
Pathway to Victory is a broadcast ministry of First Baptist Dallas.
This is an experiment we ran for PTV a couple of years ago, but we wanted to test again to see if the results still held.
We tested a second link in the email to see if it would increase engagement and down funnel donations.
experiments.keyLearnings:
The key learnings from this experiment are as follows:

1. The addition of a second link in the email did not result in a higher conversion rate. In fact, the treatment group saw a decrease in clicks for all traffic, implying that subscribers did not engage more with the email when presented with an additional link.

2. The control group, which did not have the second link, outperformed the treatment group in terms of clicks. Although this result is not statistically significant, it suggests that the original email format may be more effective in driving engagement.

3 Of those who received the treatment only 37% clicked the first link and 63% clicked the second link.

4 Donations saw a 0.9% increase with a level of confidence 4.5%.

 

Based on these results, the presence of a second link did not impact engagement or donations.

experiments.title: How adding an image of the book impacted revenue for nondonors
experiments.researchQuestion: We believe that by including an image of the book for nondonors will achieve a higher revenue impact because of the visual appeal of the book.
experiments.summary:
Ended On: 10/30/2023
Pathway to Victory is a broadcast ministry of First Dallas.
Each month PTV promotes a new premium for the month. For this month we decided to retest using an image of the book to see if it would increase revenue.
experiments.keyLearnings:
The key learning from this experiment is that including an image of the book for non-donors did not achieve a higher revenue impact. In fact, the treatment group saw a slight decrease in revenue compared to the control group. However, since the statistical level of confidence is below 85%, these results cannot be considered valid.

In future experimentation, it is important to ensure that the sample size is sufficient to achieve a higher level of statistical confidence. Additionally, it may be helpful to further explore the reasons behind the decrease in revenue for the treatment group. This could involve conducting qualitative research or gathering feedback from participants to better understand their preferences and reactions to the image of the book.

Overall, the learnings from this experiment suggest that including an image of the book may not be the most effective strategy for increasing revenue. It would be beneficial to consider other potential variables or elements to test in future experiments in order to optimize revenue impact.

experiments.title: How a recurring tab impacted conversions
experiments.researchQuestion: We believe that by adding a monthly option for web donors will achieve higher conversion rate because there is additional give options and clarity in the type of gift.
experiments.summary:
Timeframe: 01/10/2024 - 01/26/2024
Pathway to Victory is the broadcast ministry of First Dallas.
PTV’s offers are primarily various books and premiums with donations. Our January emphasis was to increase the number of recurring donors. With this in mind, we decided to try adding a recurring tab to normal offers to see if we could passively acquire recurring donors by just having the option available.
experiments.keyLearnings:
Note: treatment generated 0 recurring gifts
The key learning from this experiment is that adding a monthly option for web donors did not achieve a higher conversion rate. In fact, the treatment group saw a decrease in transactions compared to the control group. However, it is important to note that the statistical level of confidence is below 85%, meaning the results are not considered valid and should not be used for future decision-making.
Based on this, it is necessary to conduct further experiments with a larger sample size or different variations to determine the effectiveness of adding a recurring tab for web donors. It is also important to consider other factors that may have influenced the results, such as the timing of the experiment or the specific offer being presented.
Future experimentation should focus on gathering more data and conducting rigorous A/B testing to determine the impact of different variables on conversion rates. It may also be beneficial to analyze other aspects of the donor journey, such as the effectiveness of different messaging or marketing channels, to gain a deeper understanding of donor behavior and preferences. Additionally, seeking feedback from donors and analyzing their motivations and decision-making processes can provide valuable insights for future experiments.

experiments.title: How making the donor the hero and using a empathetic, conversational tone on a content offer donation page impacts donor conversion
experiments.researchQuestion: We believe that making the donor the hero and using a conversational, empathetic tone on a content offer donation page will increase donor conversion
experiments.summary:
Timeframe: 08/24/2023 - 09/22/2023
Focus on the Family uses many free video series offers to acquire subscribers and donors. When reviewing their donation page template, we found that the copy was both long and impersonal. Its tone also talked a lot about the organization and less about the donor’s impact. It included phrases like, “we’ve done the math” that could potentially make visitors feel like Focus was the expert and their thoughts weren’t as valuable.
Users also mentioned that the template lacked a more authentic and conversational tone. We decided to redesign the donation page to be more impact-driven and conversational, hypothesizing that this would increase the perceived value of the offer while also making it easier to read.
We chose to run this on several offers. This experiment is for their Bringing Biblical Truth to a Broken Culture offer. You can review the results of a similar experiment on their Navigating Adolescence offer here.
experiments.keyLearnings:
After running this experiment for a month, we found no statistically significant impact on donor conversion from the treatment for this offer. We did however see an increase in donor conversion on the Navigating Adolescence offer we tested during the same time frame at a higher, though not quite valid level of confidence When looking at recurring donor conversion in the Broken Culture offer, we found a 34% increase but only at a 72% level of confidence so this wasn't valid. We will continue to test donation page layouts and language on a per-offer basis to see which designs will increase donor conversion.
Additional data can be found below:
One-Time Transactions: treatment led to a -1.6% decrease in donor conversion w/ a 11.9% LOCRecurring Transactions: treatment had a 37.9% increase in donor conversion w/ a 44.9% LOCVWO captured approximately $1,300 more revenue from the control than the treatment.

experiments.title: How the presentation of a digital resource on a landing page impacts email sign ups
experiments.researchQuestion: We believe that presenting the resource in a digital form for landing page viewers will achieve an increased email sign up rate because they have clarity in the way they will be able to use the digital resource. .
experiments.summary:
Timeframe: 09/08/2023 - 09/21/2023
Dallas Theological Seminary has participated in North Texas Giving Day each year. From previous testing, they know that priming their housefile with a content offer has historically lead to increased donors and revenue. This year, they wanted to test if presenting the eBook in a different method on the landing page would impact email sign ups.
Historically with eBooks, they have created an image that represents the eBook as a 3D eBook preview—more like an actual book. They decided to test this against a mock-up of the eBook on an iPad.
experiments.keyLearnings:
The key learning from this experiment is that presenting the resource in a digital form on the landing page resulted in a slight increase in email signups compared to the traditional 3D eBook preview. However, it is important to note that the statistical level of confidence is only 40.1%, which is below the acceptable threshold of 85%.
Based on these results, it is not advisable to make any definitive conclusions or decisions regarding the effectiveness of presenting the eBook in a digital form. The results of this experiment should not be used for future decision-making.
In order to obtain more reliable and valid results, it is recommended to conduct further testing with a larger sample size and a higher statistical level of confidence. By doing so, the nonprofit can gather more accurate and conclusive data to determine the most effective method of presenting the eBook on the landing page.
DTS will be testing this again during Calendar Year End on their Christmas Devotional, which has historically gotten a significantly higher rate of traffic and should be able to give us a better sample size.

experiments.title: How concise copy and additional clarity impacted donor conversions for a content offer
experiments.researchQuestion: We believe that simplifying the language, reordering information for a more logical flow, and emphasizing key points for visitors will achieve an increase in clarity and comprehension.
experiments.summary:
Timeframe: 01/05/2024 - 01/16/2024
In an effort to increase the donor conversion rate on IMB’s Prayer Calendar campaign, we decided to run a copy test to condense some of the copy. Our hypothesis was that the more concise the copy, the higher level of comprehension for the potential donor therefore, leading them to the donation form quicker.
We believe that simplifying the language, reordering information for a more logical flow, and emphasizing key points will lead to increased clarity and comprehension. As a result, we believe the treatment version will outperform the control version in terms of donor understanding and response, leading to a higher conversion rate.
experiments.keyLearnings:
The key learning from this experiment is that simplifying the language, reordering information for a more logical flow, and emphasizing key points in the copy had a very slight lift in the number of transactions. While the treatment version of the campaign showed a 1.1% increase in transactions, the statistical level of confidence was only 3.1%, falling below the recommended threshold of 95% for validity.
Furthermore, we saw a a drop of 35.3% in revenue between the control and treatment at a 12.1% level of confidence. While this did not validate, it is important to consider in future decision making that IMB's donors give less when they are given less value proposition, and more concise copy may lead to a lower average gift.
In future experimentation, it is important to carry out the A/B test for a longer duration to gather a larger sample size and increase the statistical power. This will help establish a more reliable and accurate conclusion about the effectiveness of the treatment version. Additionally, it may be beneficial to further refine and optimize the treatment version based on the insights gained from this experiment. Overall, the learnings from this experiment should be used as a starting point for further iterations and testing to drive improved results.

experiments.title: How Facebook creative impacted donor conversions
experiments.researchQuestion: We believe that by changing the creative to the authors photo for Facebook viewers will achieve a higher conversion rate because the authors are well known to the Hoover audience.
experiments.summary:
Timeframe: 07/26/2023 - 08/25/2023
The Hoover Institution is the public policy think tank for Stanford University.
Hoover runs many offers through Facebook and therefore we wanted to test a new approach to creative for ebooks. Hoover has many well known personalities that write the offers shared so we decided to test the standard 3d image of the offer vs an image with the authors photo.
experiments.keyLearnings:
The key learnings from this experiment are:

1. The treatment (using the authors' photo as the creative) resulted in a decrease in purchases for all traffic, indicating that it did not perform as well as the control (using a standard 3d image).

2. The level of confidence for the decrease in purchases is only 3.2%, which is below the acceptable threshold of 85%. This means that the results cannot be considered valid and should not be used for future decision making.

3. However, there were significant increases in other metrics such as clicks and complete registrations. Clicks increased by 184% with a 99% level of confidence, and complete registrations increased by 225% with a 95% level of confidence.

4. Cost per click and cost per registration also saw significant increases, with a 424% increase and a 376% increase respectively, both with a level of confidence of 100%.

Based on these findings, future experimentation could focus on leveraging the authors' photos in the creative for specific goals such as increasing clicks and complete registrations. However, it should be noted that the decrease in purchases suggests that using authors' photos may not be effective in driving actual conversions. Therefore, further experiments should be conducted to explore the impact on conversion rates and validity of results. Additionally, it could be valuable to assess the overall impact on campaign performance and return on investment when considering changes to the creative strategy.

experiments.title: How added value of a premium impacted donations
experiments.researchQuestion: We believe that by adding more information about the premium for potential donors will achieve a higher conversion rate because there is clarity around what they will receive.
experiments.summary:
Timeframe: 08/04/2023 - 09/15/2023
Pathway to Victory is a broadcast ministry of First Baptist Dallas.
PTV promotes various premiums each month, in an effort to increase donor conversion we decided to test adding additional copy about the offer on the donation page to drive response.
experiments.keyLearnings:
The key takeaway from this experiment is that adding more information about the premium on the donation page led to a slight increase (2.7%) in transactions compared to the control group. While this increase is statistically significant (27.6% level of confidence), it falls below the recommended threshold (85%) for valid results.
Therefore, the results of this experiment should not be used for future decision making. It is essential to conduct further experiments with a larger sample size or make changes to the experiment setup to achieve a higher level of confidence.
In future experimentation, it is recommended to focus on increasing the sample size to improve statistical significance. Additionally, evaluating other factors that could potentially impact donor conversion, such as the clarity of the premium offer, could provide valuable insights for future optimization efforts.
Overall, this experiment highlights the need for rigorous statistical analysis and a larger sample size to obtain reliable results for decision making in the future.

experiments.title: How a low-to-high gift array affects donor conversion (Why the Bible)
experiments.researchQuestion:
We believe that a low-to-high gift array for users on the (Why the Bible) instant donation page will achieve an increase in donor conversion rate because highlighting a more entry-level gift amount will help increase first time donors and those unable to gift a large amount but who want to support.
because  highlighting a more entry-level gift amount will help increase first time donors and those unable to gift a large amount but who want to support.
experiments.summary:
Timeframe: 05/11/2023 - 06/05/2023
Reasons to Believe orders their gift arrays from highest ask to lowest; the assumption being that starting with the larger gift will increase the average gift. However, we hypothesized that for instant donation pages on offers specifically—where perhaps a user isn’t already familiar with RTB— a $250 gift might be a daunting place to start.  We believe that lowering the barrier to entry will increase the number of gifts. This will lead to an overall increase in revenue even if the average gift is lowered due to the increase in conversion rate.
experiments.keyLearnings: The key learning from this experiment is that highlighting a more entry-level gift amount on an instant donation page can potentially increase the number of first-time donors and those who cannot gift a large amount but still want to support. This hypothesis was supported by the treatment group, which saw a 64.7% increase in revenue for all traffic. However, since the statistical level of confidence was below 85%, the results cannot be considered valid for future decision making. However, testing this same treatment on a page with greater sample size could help this hypothesis validate.

experiments.title: How a low-to-high gift array affected donor conversion (Noah’s Flood)
experiments.researchQuestion: We believe that a low-to-high gift array for users on the Noah’s Flood instant donation page will achieve an increase in donor conversion rate because highlighting a more entry-level gift amount will increase first time donors and those unable to gift a large amount but who want to support.
experiments.summary:
Timeframe: 05/11/2023 - 06/05/2023
Reasons to Believe orders their gift arrays from highest ask to lowest; the assumption being that starting with the larger gift will increase the average gift. However, we hypothesized that for instant donation pages on offers specifically—where perhaps a user isn’t already familiar with RTB— a $250 gift might be a daunting place to start. We believe that lowering the barrier to entry will increase the number of gifts. This will lead to an overall increase in revenue even if the average gift is lowered due to the increase in conversion rate.
experiments.keyLearnings:
The experiment ran for 24 days, and the results showed that the treatment group saw a 48.1% increase in revenue for all traffic with a statistical level of confidence of 73.4%. Though these results cannot be considered valid.
From this experiment, the key learnings are that highlighting a more entry-level gift amount increased the conversion rate for first-time donors and those who are unable to gift a large amount but want to support the cause. Furthermore, it is evident that the organization should continue testing and experimenting to find out the ideal donation page design and gift array.
In future experimentation, the organization should increase the sample size, use a longer testing period and improve the test variation to ensure higher statistical validity

experiments.title: How a brief, to the point email impacted revenue
experiments.researchQuestion: We believe that by shortening the email to the important parts for emails subscribers will achieve a higher conversion rate because there is increased urgency and less copy.
experiments.summary:
Ended On: 08/18/2023
PTV is the global media ministry of First Baptist Dallas.
Each month PTV promotes a book written by Dr. Robert Jeffress. For August, Dr Jeffress release a new book that was a available for only a couple weeks. In an effort to see if we could drive results in the final days, we tested a shorter, straight to the point email vs the standard descriptive version.
experiments.keyLearnings:
The key learning from this experiment is that the shorter, straight to the point email was more effective in driving conversions and generating revenue compared to the standard descriptive email. Despite the statistical level of confidence being below the threshold of 85%, the treatment still showed a significant 31.1% increase in revenue for all traffic.

In future experimentation, it would be advisable to continue exploring the use of shorter and more concise emails for promotional purposes. This experiment suggests that there is potential in leveraging increased urgency and reducing the amount of copy to drive better results.

However, it is important to note that the results of this particular test should not be used for future decision making due to the low statistical level of confidence. It would be beneficial to run further experiments to validate these findings and ensure their replicability before implementing them as a standard practice.

Additionally, it would be useful to analyze any qualitative feedback or customer insights to better understand the reasons behind the improved performance of the shorter email. This can inform future experiments and help refine the email strategy further.

experiments.title: How removing the main menu navigation bar from a donation page effected conversion rate
experiments.researchQuestion: We believe that removing the main navigation menu from the page for potential donors will achieve a higher conversion rate because this would reduce decision friction so the donor motivation would not break by having too many decisions to make.
experiments.summary:
Timeframe: 06/20/2023 - 07/02/2023
This ministry client offers different ministries that a donor can choose to give towards. In this experiment, this donation page contributes to providing biblical resources to international prisoners. We were interested in testing decision friction on the donation page. We believed that removing the main navigation menu at the top of the page could reduce decision friction. Decision friction gives the donor too many options to consider which breaks the train of thought they had for giving. This is something we wanted to reduce and could potentially lead to a higher conversion rate.
Between the control and treatment donation page, the only difference was that the control page had the menu navigation whereas the treatment page did not. This test ran as a split traffic 50/50 A/B test.
experiments.keyLearnings:
The key learning from this experiment is that removing the main navigation menu from the donation page did not lead to an increase in conversions. In fact, the treatment page saw a decrease in donations compared to the control page. However, the statistical confidence level of 23.3% is below the acceptable threshold of 85%, so these results did not validate.

Based on these findings, the presence of the menu may not have been a significant source of decision friction for donors, and removing it may have actually hindered their experience on the page.

For future experimentation, we could focus on other elements of the donation page that could be optimized to increase conversions. This could include testing different calls-to-action or simplicity the donation page copy to enhance the persuasiveness of the page. It is important to conduct further experiments with larger sample sizes and stricter statistical confidence thresholds to obtain valid and reliable results.

experiments.title: How adding an urgent countdown clock pop-up to a page impacted conversion rate
experiments.researchQuestion: We believe that by adding an urgent countdown pop-up for potential donors will achieve an increase in conversion rate because the potential donor’s motivation would increase to want to give now rather than wait.
experiments.summary:
Timeframe: 06/06/2023 - 07/02/2023
This ministry client supports international prisoners by giving them biblical resources. The page we wanted to test is a secondary page to the main supporting prisoner page. We wanted to add an urgent countdown clock pop-up to this page to drive the conversion rate. This countdown clock included a message of how the potential donor could help be the one to complete the goal of the number of prisoners that are wanted to be supported by the end of the night. The pop-up includes urgency language by proving words like “Urgent”, “time is running out”, “by tonight” to make the potential donor have a higher motivation to give now. The control page was the original secondary page and the treatment page had the urgent pop-up.
This test was run as a 50/50 A/B test.
experiments.keyLearnings:
The key learnings from this experiment are that adding an urgent countdown pop-up can have a positive impact on donation conversion rates. The treatment group saw a significant increase in donations compared to the control group. However, it is important to note that the statistical level of confidence is below the recommended threshold of 95%. This means that the results of this experiment cannot be considered validated.

In future experimentation, it is recommended to run a larger sample size or run the experiment for a longer duration to increase the statistical power and achieve a higher level of confidence. Additionally, it may also be worth considering testing an urgent pop-up compared to a normal support pop-up to see if the urgent pop-up would still produce higher conversion rates.

experiments.title: How Pulling Mobile Give Button Out of Menu Impacts Donations
experiments.researchQuestion: We believe that making the donate button visible in the navigation bar for for mobile users will achieve an increase in donations  because  the increased visibility eliminates friction for those who either might not have known where to give.
experiments.summary:
Timeframe: 05/30/2023 - 07/03/2023
RTB’s Mobile view hides the Donate button within the “hamburger” menu. So, unlike the desktop view, a user has to open the menu then select “Donate” from the button in order to make a gift rather than just clicking a button in the navigation. We believe that updating this, so that the give button is displayed for mobile view will increase donations.
Additionally, we ran this test during fiscal year end know that there would be more website traffic and motivation would be higher.
experiments.keyLearnings:
The key learning from this experiment is that making the donate button visible in the navigation bar for mobile users resulted in a 35.1% increase in donations.
